name: Stockholm
profile: Sweden's capital and largest city — government, banking and finance, and the country's deepest technology and startup market, alongside life sciences, media and professional services. Anchored by Stockholm University, KTH Royal Institute of Technology and Karolinska Institutet with Karolinska University Hospital. The most international job market in the country, and where English-first roles are most common.

name: Gothenburg
profile: Sweden's second city, on the west coast — the centre of the automotive industry around Volvo and its supplier base, plus manufacturing and engineering, and Scandinavia's largest port driving logistics and shipping. Home to the University of Gothenburg, Chalmers University of Technology and Sahlgrenska University Hospital.

name: Malmö
profile: Sweden's third city, in the far south and linked to Copenhagen by the Öresund Bridge — a logistics, construction and services hub with a young, unusually international population, and part of the Öresund life-science cluster. Home to Malmö University, with Lund University nearby. Its cross-border labour market means some residents work in Denmark, which changes the language calculation.

name: Uppsala
profile: A university city just north of Stockholm — Uppsala University, founded in 1477 and the oldest in the Nordic countries, plus the Swedish University of Agricultural Sciences (SLU) and Uppsala University Hospital. Strong in life sciences, pharmaceuticals, biotech and public research, with commuter links into the Stockholm job market.

name: Linköping
profile: An engineering and research city in the east — aerospace and defence around Saab, plus IT, software and simulation clusters at Linköping Science Park. Home to Linköping University and Linköping University Hospital, with a job market weighted toward technical and academic roles.
